trend_description.description: Language used to describe change over time in charts or graphs.

comparison.description: Language used to compare values, categories, or groups.

data_reference.description: Language used to refer to charts, figures, categories, and time points.

quantity_expression.description: Language used to describe amounts, percentages, proportions, and numbers.

sentence_pattern.description: Reusable grammatical patterns for Task 1 reporting.

reporting_function.description: Communicative functions specific to IELTS Writing Task 1.

items.structure: There was a notable and consistent increase in sth over the period.
items.example: Overall, there was a notable and consistent increase in the number of both men and women participating in further education across the three periods.
In 1970/71, men formed the largest group.

items.structure: Sth became the majority/minority in a category by a certain time.
items.example: who became the majority in this category by 1990/91.

items.structure: Overall, there was a notable trend in sth over the periods.
items.example: Overall, there was a notable and consistent increase in the number of both men and women participating in further education across the three periods.

items.structure: The most significant trend observed was sth.
items.example: The most significant trend observed was the substantial rise in part-time study, particularly among women.

items.structure: Sth decreased slightly to a certain amount.
items.example: while the number of part-time male students decreased slightly to about 900 thousand.

items.structure: Conversely, the scenario for one group showed significant differences.
items.example: Conversely, the scenario for women showed significant differences.

items.structure: Sth surged to nearly a certain amount.
items.example: By 1990/91, part-time study among women surged to nearly 1,100 thousand.
